blog.title: The Journal
blog.subtitle: Reflections on architecture, the evolution of materiality, and the quiet luxury of the handmade.

posts.title: What's Changing in Microcement Design for 2026
posts.excerpt: Warmer palettes, continuous surfaces in commercial spaces, and matte finishes that hide daily wear better. What's actually changing this year.
content: The biggest shift we're seeing this year is in color. For the last few years, cool greys dominated almost every project that came through our studio. In 2026, clients are asking for warmer tones: sand, clay, soft ochre, even the occasional dusty pink. Cold minimalism is giving way to something that still feels minimal but reads as lived-in rather than sterile.

On the commercial side, we're getting more requests from hospitality and retail clients for continuous mineral surfaces that run from the floor up the walls and onto custom furniture. A single material across a whole space used to be mostly a residential thing. Now hotels and shops want that same continuity, partly because it photographs well and partly because one surface is easier to maintain than a patchwork of finishes.

Low-VOC certification used to be a selling point. Now it's close to a baseline expectation, especially for bedrooms, nurseries, and healthcare spaces. What's changed more is the finish itself: instead of high-gloss polished microcement, more clients are choosing matte or lightly textured surfaces that hide fingerprints and water marks better in daily use.

posts.title: The Rhythm of the Hand
posts.excerpt: Why hand-troweled surfaces look different from machine-finished ones, and whether it matters.
content: Microcement is a mix of minerals and polymers, but the finish depends on the person applying it. A hand-troweled surface carries small variations a poured floor or factory tile never will: the pressure of the trowel, the angle of each pass, the rhythm of the hand doing the work.

Those variations are why the same color can feel different from one room to the next, even using the same material. Most architecture is moving toward machine-perfect finishes, so a surface with this kind of texture stands out. It isn't a flaw to sand away. It's a record of how the surface was made.
Our artisans fall into a steady rhythm while they work, and that rhythm shows up in the final texture. You can see it in the right light, and you can feel it under your palm.

posts.title: Exploring Mineral Tonality
posts.excerpt: The same mineral surface looks warmer at dusk than at noon. Here's why.
content: The color of a mineral surface never sits still. We use raw earthen pigments, so the tone shifts as the light in the room changes through the day.
Take our Bone white: warm at sunset, almost cool and architectural under midday light. That shift isn't an accident, it's part of how we choose pigments in the first place.

When we work with architects, we talk about the surface as something that responds to its setting rather than sitting flat against it. Picking the right pigment base means thinking about which way a room faces and what the light does there over the course of a day.

posts.title: Bathroom Renovation Without Demolition
posts.excerpt: How to redo a bathroom without removing a single tile using Luxury Concrete® technology.
content: A typical bathroom renovation means weeks of demolition, dust everywhere, and walls torn back to the studs. Microcement skips most of that: it's a thin mineral coating that goes directly over your existing tiles.

That works because Luxury Concrete® microcement bonds hard to almost any stable surface, including old tile. Skip the demolition and you cut waste and the project timeline, and you still end up with a seamless, spa-like finish.
There's also a practical upside beyond looks. No grout lines means nothing to scrub, and no hidden gaps for moisture and mold to build up in.

posts.title: The Definitive Guide: Microcement on Tiles
posts.excerpt: Applying microcement over existing tiles takes more prep than most people expect. This is what we do.
content: Can you apply microcement over old tiles? Yes, as long as the tiles are sound, not loose or cracked. The big advantage is a modern, grout-free look without tearing out the floor or raising it by much.

We start by diamond-grinding the tile to open up the surface, then apply a double layer of epoxy primer with fiberglass mesh underneath. That combination stops 'ghosting', where old grout lines slowly become visible again through the new surface months later.
We use base mortars chosen specifically to absorb structural movement under the new layer. The finished surface is about 3mm thick, but it holds up like a high-end architectural concrete.

posts.title: Sculptural Silence: Microcement Furniture Trends 2026
posts.excerpt: More furniture is being specified in mineral materials this year. Some reasons why that's happening.
content: The line between furniture and architecture keeps getting thinner. More designers are skipping modular, assembled pieces in favor of single monolithic objects that look like they grew out of the floor.

Microcement works well for this: cube-shaped coffee tables, cantilevered dining surfaces, sideboards with no visible seams. It holds sharp geometric edges but still feels warm to the touch, closer to stone than to concrete.

The look that's gaining ground pairs these heavy mineral shapes with soft textiles, like a wool throw draped over a poured concrete bench. A piece like that doesn't just sit in a room, it changes how the room feels.

products.title: The Luxury Concrete® Collection
products.subtitle: Kuusdisain — official LUXURY CONCRETE® reseller in Estonia. Professional microcement systems for every surface.

concrete.name: Concrete
concrete.description: Concrete is Luxury Concrete®'s two-component microcement family: Concrete Base prepares the substrate, Concrete Wall and Concrete Floor finish walls and floors, Concrete Stone is built for outdoor use and Concrete Pool for pools. Every finish is mixed with Concrete Resin acrylic resin.

features.title: Complete system
features.desc: Concrete Base prepares the substrate; Wall, Floor, Stone and Pool deliver the finish — one family for every surface.

features.title: 3 grain sizes
features.desc: Concrete Base is available in L, XL and XXL grain for coarser or finer preparation.

features.title: Indoor and outdoor
features.desc: Concrete Stone and Concrete Pool are formulated for exterior use and constant water contact.

details.maintenance: Clean with pH-neutral products. Avoid abrasive scrubbing pads.
details.philosophy: A two-component microcement built for high adhesion and mechanical resistance — Spanish manufacturer Luxury Concrete®'s craftsmanship.

monocrete.name: Monocrete
monocrete.description: Monocrete is a one-component microcement family containing resin powder, needing only water to mix. Monocrete Base prepares the substrate; Monocrete Wall and Monocrete Floor deliver a silky finish for walls and floors.

features.title: Just add water
features.desc: Resin powder is pre-blended — add water and mix. Easier to store and transport than two-component systems.

features.title: 3 grain sizes
features.desc: Monocrete Base is available in L, XL and XXL grain depending on the substrate.

features.title: Silky finish
features.desc: Monocrete Wall and Floor deliver a soft, even, naturally light finish.

details.maintenance: Clean with pH-neutral products. Avoid abrasive scrubbing pads.
details.philosophy: An evolution of tradition — a one-component system that simplifies installation without sacrificing quality.

easycret.name: Easycret
easycret.description: Easycret is a ready-to-use microcement paste built for faster, simpler work than traditional microcement. Easycret Thin suits fine finishes, Easycret Basic gives a coarser, natural texture, and Easycret Medium is built for a continuous floor finish.

features.title: Fast application
features.desc: A ready-made paste that only needs pigment — saves time compared to hand-mixed systems.

features.title: High adhesion
features.desc: Bonds to most common substrates: tile, terrazzo, plaster, cement.

features.title: Three grain sizes
features.desc: Thin for a fine finish, Basic for a natural texture, Medium for a continuous floor finish.

details.maintenance: Clean with pH-neutral products. Avoid abrasive scrubbing pads.
details.philosophy: Ideal for fast renovation work without compromising on the quality of a ready-made paste.

concrete-pox.name: Concrete Pox
concrete-pox.tagline: Epoxy microcement with higher hardness and waterproofing
concrete-pox.description: Concrete Pox is a water-based epoxy microcement combining aggregate and a water-based epoxy resin. With a Shore hardness of 80–87, it suits high-traffic floors and walls: garages, warehouses, shops and offices. Available in four grain sizes and 16 colours.

features.title: High hardness
features.desc: Shore hardness 80–87 — resistant to impact and everyday abrasion.

features.title: Waterproof
features.desc: Enhanced waterproofing prepares the surface for the most demanding moisture conditions.

features.title: 4 grain sizes
features.desc: Extra and Basic for preparation, Medium and Thin for the finish coat.

specs.label: System
specs.value: 2-component (A: aggregate + cycloaliphatic amine; B: water-based epoxy resin)

details.maintenance: Clean with pH-neutral products. Avoid abrasive scrubbing pads.
details.philosophy: A premium epoxy microcement for the most demanding spaces, where abrasion resistance is critical.

limecrete.name: Limecrete
limecrete.description: Limecrete is a two-component lime-based microcement bringing together tadelakt's handcrafted finish and visible-concrete aesthetics. Extra, Basic, Medium and Thin grain sizes cover both preparation and finishing, indoors and out.

features.title: Lime-based
features.desc: A traditional, more sustainable formulation carrying tadelakt's two-thousand-year heritage.

features.title: Handcrafted finish
features.desc: Can be applied "fresh on fresh" for unique, personalised textures.

features.title: 4 grain sizes
features.desc: Extra and Basic for prep and finishing, Medium and Thin for finer finishes.

specs.label: Application
specs.value: Walls and floors, indoors and outdoors

details.maintenance: Clean with pH-neutral products. Avoid abrasive scrubbing pads.
details.philosophy: Tradition and technology hand in hand — Morocco's tadelakt heritage with modern performance.

metallic.name: Metallic finishes
metallic.tagline: Colorcrete Metal, Oxid Metal and True Metal
metallic.description: Luxury Concrete®'s metallic range spans three technologies: Colorcrete Metal (Gemstone and Glowing collections) for a shimmering, reflective effect; Oxid Metal, which reacts with a water-based oxidant for an authentic patinated result; and True Metal, a two-component high-metal-content finish in brass, bronze, copper, aluminium and iridium tones.

features.title: Three technologies
features.desc: Colorcrete Metal for shimmer, Oxid Metal for oxidation, True Metal for a genuine metallic feel — each with its own character.

features.title: Oxidation effect
features.desc: Colorcrete Oxidant accelerates the rusting/patina process on Oxid Metal and True Metal finishes.

features.title: Wide tonal range
features.desc: Brass, bronze, copper, aluminium, iridium, amber, carnelian, diamond, ruby and more.

specs.label: System
specs.value: Colorcrete Metal (pigment), Oxid Metal (water solution), True Metal (2-component)

details.maintenance: Clean with pH-neutral products. Avoid abrasive scrubbing pads.
details.philosophy: Metal that lives and changes over time — a luxurious accent for any room.

primers.name: Primers
primers.tagline: Primacrete primers and the IMPOXY® moisture barrier
primers.description: Primacrete is Luxury Concrete®'s range of primers and bonding promoters, ensuring optimal substrate preparation before applying microcement. IMPOXY® is a two-component epoxy system used as a vapour or moisture barrier.

features.title: For every substrate
features.desc: Primacrete ABS for absorbent surfaces (drywall, plaster), Primacrete Plus for non-absorbent (marble, terrazzo, tile), Primacrete Grip to roughen a smooth surface.

features.title: Moisture barrier
features.desc: IMPOXY® blocks moisture and water vapour — 100% solids, solvent-free.

features.title: Joint filler
features.desc: Primacrete Joint is the joint filler for the Easycret ready-to-use microcement.

details.philosophy: The right base is the foundation of every microcement surface.

sealers.name: Sealers
sealers.tagline: The Concrete Finish family for protecting microcement
sealers.description: Concrete Finish is Luxury Concrete®'s range of sealers protecting microcement surfaces from wear, staining and UV exposure. Primacrete Finish is a pre-sealer that bridges the microcement and the final varnish coat.

features.title: Matt, satin or gloss
features.desc: Concrete Finish WT and WT Max offer a choice of finishes, including a slip-resistant option.

features.title: For wet areas
features.desc: Concrete Finish WT Max and WT Pool are formulated for damp rooms and pools.

features.title: Won't yellow
features.desc: Concrete Finish Extra and DSV withstand prolonged sun exposure without yellowing — suited to outdoor use.

specs.label: Application
specs.value: Final coat and protection for microcement

details.philosophy: The right sealer keeps microcement performing for years.

pigments.name: Pigments
pigments.tagline: Colorcrete BASE and MIX pigment pastes
pigments.description: Colorcrete is Luxury Concrete®'s range of pigment pastes in over 30 tones. Colorcrete BASE comprises 6 base colours (green, blue, black, red, yellow, white) that mix into Luxury Concrete's 36-colour core chart. Colorcrete MIX offers pre-dosed pigment units for a specific product and finish.

features.title: 36-colour core chart
features.desc: 6 Colorcrete BASE tones create the colours across Luxury Concrete's core collections.

features.title: Pre-dosed
features.desc: Colorcrete MIX offers precise, repeatable dosing for a specific product and finish.

features.title: Light- and alkali-resistant
features.desc: High alkali resistance and colour stability that doesn't shift with light or ageing.

details.philosophy: Every tone is a source of inspiration — pigments are the foundation of Luxury Concrete's colour palette.

care.name: Cleaning and maintenance
care.description: Luxury Concrete®'s cleaning and maintenance range keeps microcement surfaces looking their best. Concrete CLEAN and Concrete PRO CLEAN remove heavy, stubborn stains; Concrete WAX and Concrete PRO WAX restore shine and reinforce surface protection.

features.title: Eco-friendly
features.desc: Concrete CLEAN is biodegradable and effective even on heavy soiling.

features.title: Restores shine
features.desc: Concrete WAX and PRO WAX give the surface a metallic sheen that won't yellow.

features.title: Professional use
features.desc: PRO CLEAN and PRO WAX are formulated for professional deep cleaning and maintenance.

specs.label: Application
specs.value: Routine cleaning and maintenance of microcement surfaces

details.maintenance: Clean with pH-neutral products; avoid abrasive pads and acidic cleaners.
details.philosophy: Regular maintenance keeps microcement looking its best for decades.

tools.name: Tools
tools.tagline: Fibreglass mesh, steel trowels and rollers
tools.description: Professional tools for microcement installation: fibreglass mesh (50/58/160gr) to prevent cracking, flexible rubber, carbon-fibre and stainless-steel trowels for applying finishes, and texture rollers for primers and sealers.

features.title: Fibreglass mesh
features.desc: Consolidates the substrate and prevents cracking — available in three weights.

features.title: Specialised trowels
features.desc: Rubber trowel for fine-grain finishes, carbon-fibre for preparation coats, stainless Bi-Flex for the most textured surfaces.

features.title: Texture rollers
features.desc: Recommended for applying primers and sealers.

details.philosophy: The right tools matter as much as the right product.

story.title: About us
story.subtitle: We want to create surfaces that last from generation to generation and carry true craftsmanship value.

origin.title: VISION AND EXECUTION
origin.text: Our strength lies in combining two seemingly different worlds – fine artistic sensibility and decades of construction experience. The result is spaces where every detail is thought through and masterfully executed.

philosophy.title: The Master's Touch
philosophy.text: Every surface we create is a unique work of art. We do not use machines, but trust our hands. This hand-applied rhythm means that light and shadows play on the walls and floors differently every time, giving your home's interior a unique soul.

faq.title: Architectural Specification FAQ
faq.subtitle: Technical insights into the performance and application of our mineral systems.

items.question: What is the typical thickness of a Luxury Concrete® application?
items.answer: Our systems are applied in multiple calibrated layers, reaching a total thickness of 2-3mm. This ensures extreme structural density without altering existing floor levels or architectural profiles.

items.question: Is microcement compatible with advanced heating systems?
items.answer: Absolutely. Microcement possesses excellent thermal conductivity, making it an ideal surface for both hydronic and electric underfloor heating, ensuring efficient and even heat distribution.

items.question: What are the long-term maintenance requirements?
items.answer: The surface should be cleaned with pH-neutral agents to preserve the integrity of the seal. Regular maintenance ensures the patina develops gracefully while maintaining its protective properties.

items.question: Can it be applied over existing substrates like ceramic tiles?
items.answer: Yes. Our systems are engineered for universal adhesion. We prepare the existing substrate through mechanical grinding and high-performance priming, eliminating the need for costly and disruptive demolition.

business.title: Working with studios and developers
business.subtitle: We work with architects, interior studios, and developers on commercial projects. Hotels, retail spaces, restaurant fit-outs — if it needs a durable mineral surface, we can help.

hero.title: Monolithic Architectural Surfaces
hero.subtitle: Artisanal microcement systems in Estonia. Seamless mineral skins designed for architectural endurance.

philosophy.title: Materiality Defines the Atmosphere
philosophy.text: We believe surfaces should not just fill space—they should define the quiet within it. Each layer is hand-troweled, capturing a unique topography of light and shadow that evolves with the day. It is architectural precision, delivered with an artisanal soul.

methodology.title: Craftsmanship & Technology
methodology.subtitle: Precision in every layer. Our multi-stage application process ensures structural integrity and visual depth.

steps.title: Substrate Engineering
steps.text: The foundation of a flawless finish. We perform rigorous surface preparation—grinding, priming, and structural reinforcement—to ensure absolute adhesion and long-term stability.

steps.title: Artisanal Application
steps.text: The core mineral layers are hand-troweled by our master artisans. This manual process captures the unique rhythm of the applicator, creating a subtle topography that machine processes cannot replicate.

steps.title: Mineral Saturation
steps.text: The final aesthetic layers are applied with meticulous care, allowing each to cure naturally. This results in a dense mineral veil that responds dynamically to shifting light conditions.

steps.title: Molecular Impregnation
steps.text: We seal the surface with high-performance, matte topcoats. This creates a waterproof, scratch-resistant barrier that preserves the raw aesthetic while ensuring architectural endurance.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.subtitle: Guidelines for using Kuus Design studio services and website.

sections.title: 1. General Provisions
sections.content: These terms of service govern the use of the Kuus Design website and services. By using the website, you accept these terms in full.

sections.title: 2. Services
sections.content: Kuus Design offers premium microcement installation and decorative surface treatments. Each project is unique craftsmanship, and the final result may vary according to material properties.

sections.title: 3. Intellectual Property
sections.content: All content on the website, including photos, texts, and design elements, belongs to Kuus Design or is licensed to us. Copying content without permission is prohibited.

sections.title: 4. Limitation of Liability
sections.content: We do our best to ensure the accuracy of info, but we are not responsible for possible technical errors or consequences resulting from the interpretation of information.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: Your data security and privacy are our priority.

sections.title: 1. Data Collection
sections.content: We collect personal data (name, email, phone) that you voluntarily provide to us via the inquiry form so that we can offer you a personalized service.

sections.title: 2. Data Use
sections.content: We use your data only to respond to inquiries, plan projects, and communicate with you. We do not sell or share your data with third parties for marketing purposes.

sections.title: 3. Cookies
sections.content: We use cookies on the website to analyze traffic and improve user experience. You have the right to opt-out of cookies in your browser settings.

sections.title: 4. Your Rights
sections.content: You have the right at any time to access your data, request its correction or deletion by contacting us via email.

visualize_cta.title: See it in your space
visualize_cta.desc: Upload a photo of your room, tap any wall, and instantly preview Luxury Concrete® tones with realistic texture and light.

guide_luxury_concrete.title: Guide
guide_luxury_concrete.subtitle: Technical mastery of mineral surfaces.
guide_luxury_concrete.description: Luxury Concrete® is a Spanish manufacturer of microcement systems. Kuusdisain is the official LUXURY CONCRETE® reseller in Estonia — our microcement systems deliver a seamless finish for floors, walls and furniture without major construction work.

what_is.title: What is microcement?
paragraphs: Microcement — also known as micro-concrete, micro-topping, or a mineral coating — is a highly versatile polymer-modified cementitious coating that has transformed interior and exterior design. Just 1–3mm thin, it bonds directly to existing tiles, concrete, wood and most rigid surfaces, creating a seamless, grout-free result.

Luxury Concrete® is a Spanish manufacturer that controls the entire process from lab to factory to application. Every product is carefully formulated and tested for a consistent result. Kuusdisain is the official LUXURY CONCRETE® reseller in Estonia, supplying and installing their systems locally.

Luxury Concrete®'s product range — Concrete, Monocrete, Easycret, Concrete Pox, Limecrete and metallic finishes — together with nine colour collections, gives designers, architects and homeowners wide creative freedom: from raw industrial concrete to refined metallic finishes.

Microcement's thin application minimises the impact on existing structures during renovation. No demolition, no raised floor levels, no construction noise — microcement is applied directly over what's already there.

items.title: Microcement floors
items.desc: Concrete Floor, Monocrete Floor, Easycret Medium and Concrete Pox are all built for floor use. Durable, compatible with underfloor heating and available in a range of colours.

items.title: Microcement bathrooms
items.desc: Luxury Concrete® systems create a seamless bathroom surface, waterproof when sealed, with no grout lines. Applied directly over existing tiles — no demolition needed.

items.title: Microcement worktops
items.desc: Concrete Wall and Concrete Floor suit kitchen worktops. Heat resistant when sealed and available in a range of tones. A clean, seamless surface that's easy to maintain.

items.title: Outdoor microcement
items.desc: Concrete Stone and Limecrete suit terraces, patios and walkways. Weather-resistant and slip-resistant finishes for outdoor use.

items.title: Microcement walls
items.desc: Concrete Wall, Monocrete Wall, Limecrete and our metallic finishes all suit walls, each with its own character — from raw concrete to tadelakt or a metallic shimmer.

items.title: Microcement pools
items.desc: Concrete Pool delivers waterproof, durable surfaces for the pool and its surrounds. Applied directly over existing pool tiles.

versatility.title: Versatility
versatility.desc: Applicable to almost any surface — floors, walls, furniture, stairs and pools, including curved or irregular substrates.

durability.title: Durability
durability.desc: High resistance to everyday wear, impact, staining and scratching.

customization.title: 7 colour collections
customization.desc: Colorcrete, True Metal, Oxid Metal, Gemstone, Glowing, Concrete Pox and Limecrete — each with its own character.

waterproof.title: Waterproof
waterproof.desc: Naturally waterproof when sealed — ideal for bathrooms, showers, pool surrounds and kitchen worktops.

thickness.title: Minimal thickness
thickness.desc: 1–3mm total application. No significant change to floor level. No structural impact on the building.

flexibility.title: Choice of systems
flexibility.desc: Two-component, one-component or ready-to-use — choose the system that fits the project.

eco.title: Sustainable production
eco.desc: Luxury Concrete® produces as responsibly as possible, with a smaller ecological footprint.

maintenance.title: Easy maintenance
maintenance.desc: Clean with pH-neutral products. Reseal every 3–5 years.

process_steps.title: Surface preparation
process_steps.desc: The substrate must be clean, dry, sound and level. Cracks are filled and movement joints addressed. Diamond grinding opens the surface pores for optimal adhesion. This is the most critical step.

process_steps.title: Primer — Primacrete
process_steps.desc: The appropriate Primacrete product (ABS for absorbent surfaces, Plus for non-absorbent, Grip to roughen a smooth surface) is applied in one or two thin coats. Damp substrates use the IMPOXY® moisture barrier.

process_steps.title: Base coat — the system's preparation product
process_steps.desc: The first microcement layer (e.g. Concrete Base, Monocrete Base or Easycret Basic) is applied with a stainless-steel trowel, spread evenly across the surface. This layer builds structure and depth.

process_steps.title: Finish coat
process_steps.desc: The second and final layer (e.g. Concrete Wall/Floor, Monocrete Wall/Floor or Easycret Thin/Medium) is applied over the dry base coat. This determines the final colour, texture and smoothness.

process_steps.title: Pre-sealer — Primacrete Finish
process_steps.desc: A pore-filling pre-sealer bridges the microcement and the final varnish coat, reinforcing hardness and evenness.

process_steps.title: Sealing — Concrete Finish
process_steps.desc: The appropriate Concrete Finish varnish (WT, WT Max, One, DSV, WT Pool or Extra) is applied in a matt, satin or gloss finish. Provides waterproofing and long-term durability. The surface is usable 24–48h after the sealer cures.

guide_luxury_concrete.finishes_intro: Luxury Concrete®'s final coat (the Concrete Finish range) is available in three sheen levels. The finish choice affects the surface's final look, sheen level and maintenance needs.

finishes_list.title: Matt
finishes_list.desc: The most popular finish for luxury interiors. A flat, light-absorbing surface that emphasises texture and colour depth. Ideal for walls and residential floors.

finishes_list.title: Satin
finishes_list.desc: A soft, medium-sheen finish that adds warmth and subtle brightness. The most versatile finish — suits floors, walls and worktops alike.

finishes_list.title: Gloss
finishes_list.desc: High reflectivity for a bold, contemporary statement. Best for feature walls and reception areas where maximum visual impact is wanted.
